title: Discovery Service: FastAPI Application Foundation, Configuration Management, and Service Structure
description: Bootstrap the Discovery Service FastAPI application with async architecture, Pydantic v2 settings, router structure, middleware (request ID, correlation ID, structured logging), standardized error handling (RFC 7807 problem+json), OpenAPI tagging, and versioned endpoints at /api/v1. This is the structural foundation that all subsequent Discovery Service tasks build upon.
objectives: Stable async FastAPI foundation supporting multiple routers and admin UI
Deterministic per-env configuration via Pydantic Settings with no secret leakage
Request observability from the very first version

steps: Step 1 - Application Factory: Create create_app() factory in services/discovery/src/discovery/app.py. Instantiate FastAPI(title='Discovery Service', version='0.1.0', docs_url='/docs', openapi_url='/openapi.json'). Register all routers under prefix /api/v1. Add lifespan context manager (startup: init DB pool, init chain client; shutdown: close DB pool).

Step 2 - Settings (Pydantic BaseSettings): In src/discovery/config.py. DiscoverySettings: database_url: SecretStr, redis_url: SecretStr, jwt_issuer_key_id: str, jwt_issuer_did: str, env: Literal['dev','test','prod'] = 'dev', allowed_cors_origins: list[str] = [], blockchain_rpc_url: str, chain_id: int = 31337, secret_storage_backend: Literal['local','vault'] = 'local'. Load from environment variables. Validate at startup — fail with clear message if DATABASE_URL missing.

Step 3 - Router Structure: In src/discovery/api/v1/: routers/__init__.py, routers/health.py, routers/apps.py, routers/services.py, routers/sentinels.py, routers/enrollments.py, routers/credentials.py, routers/revocations.py, routers/status_lists.py, routers/chain.py, routers/audit.py. Each router registered with its prefix and tags in app.py.

Step 4 - Middleware Stack (order matters): (1) TrustedHostMiddleware (HTTPS redirect in non-dev), (2) RequestMiddleware: generate/propagate X-Request-ID and X-Correlation-ID, (3) RequestLoggingMiddleware from common.telemetry.middleware: log method, path, status, duration_ms, (4) CompressionMiddleware (gzip). Middleware defined in src/discovery/middleware/.

Step 5 - Error Handling (RFC 7807 Problem+JSON): Create ProblemDetail Pydantic model: type (URI), title, status, detail, instance (request path), request_id. Register exception handlers: RequestValidationError → 422 with details, HTTPException → use status code, generic Exception → 500 with opaque detail (no stack trace exposure). Content-Type: application/problem+json.

Step 6 - Dependency Injection Setup: Create src/discovery/dependencies.py. Provide: get_db() — AsyncSession from pool, get_settings() — cached DiscoverySettings, get_redis() — Redis connection pool. Used with Depends() on all router endpoints.

Step 7 - Health Endpoints: GET /health/live → {status: 'ok', service: 'discovery'}. GET /health/ready → checks DB (SELECT 1), Redis (PING), optional chain RPC (eth_blockNumber). Returns 200 if ready, 503 if not. These are K8s probe endpoints.

Step 8 - Secure Headers Middleware: Add security headers on every response: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer, Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000 (non-dev only). Use starlette middleware.

Step 9 - CORS Configuration: CORSMiddleware with allow_origins=settings.allowed_cors_origins, allow_methods=['GET','POST','PATCH','DELETE'], allow_headers=['Authorization','Content-Type','X-Request-ID'], allow_credentials=True. Default: empty allowed_origins (all blocked). Trust only explicitly configured UI origins.

Step 10 - Entry Point: src/discovery/main.py: import uvicorn, create app, run with host=0.0.0.0, port=8000, log_config=None (use structlog). uvicorn access logs disabled — replaced by RequestLoggingMiddleware.

problem_detail_example.title: Validation Error
problem_detail_example.detail: Field 'env' must be one of: dev, test, prod

error_codes.description: Resource already exists or state conflict

security_constraints: Stack traces MUST NOT appear in error responses — wrap all unhandled exceptions and log internally
X-Request-ID generated server-side if not provided — client-provided values accepted only if UUID4 format valid
CORS allow_origins MUST be empty by default — require explicit configuration per environment
docs_url (/docs) MUST be disabled in production environments — set to None if env == 'prod'
Pydantic SecretStr for all sensitive settings — never expose raw values in logs or error messages

acceptance_criteria: Discovery starts and serves OpenAPI UI at /docs in dev mode
GET /health/ready returns 503 if DB is unavailable
Unhandled exception returns 500 problem+json without stack trace
CORS blocks requests from unlisted origins
